sentences.quote: The synthesis of Section synthesis closed at the TQC-foundation layer but flagged a downstream obligation: the substrate ships the Hayden-Preskill code structure on a horizon MTC and the Drinfeld-centre toric-code/D(S_3) anchor, but did not ship the two further claims required to upgrade ``MTC infrastructure'' into ``machine-checked universal-gate-set machinery'' for Fibonacci anyons on the SU(2)_k Chern-Simons MTC: (i) that the Fibonacci-braid image is dense in SU(2), and (ii) that targets in SU(2) can be approximated to precision epsilon by braid words of length polylogarithmic in 1/epsilon. The first claim is F.21 (Freedman-Larsen-Wang 2002 Lemma F.21); the second is the quantitative Solovay-Kitaev theorem of Dawson and Nielsen, refining the original existence-form theorem of Solovay (unpublished, 1995) and Kitaev. The substrate now ships both at zero \texttt{sorry} on the headline statements, with one explicit calibration disclosure on the tightest-epsilon regime of a constructive Path-A variant noted in Section 9.4.
sentences.agent_notes: Section preamble (lines 826-847). Citation backing verified — three bibkeys present in CITATION_REGISTRY (round-3 reconciliation block:D4:r1:3 closed at lines 7201/7218/7235 of citations.py; KitaevShenVyalyi2002 also in registry per prior r3).

sentences.quote: This addition supersedes the corresponding bullets in synthesis 'Open problems' and synthesis 'What this paper is not' for the Fibonacci-universality question; the doublon-SWAP adiabatic-drag identification remains open as previously noted.
sentences.agent_notes: Methodology / cross-reference to earlier §; no extractable verifiable claim.

sentences.quote: Let \mathrm{BraidGroup}\,3 denote the three-strand braid group with generators \sigma_1,\sigma_2. The Fibonacci representation \rho_{\mathrm{Fib}}:\mathrm{BraidGroup}\,3 \to \mathrm{SU}(2) sends \sigma_1,\sigma_2 to a pair of non-commuting SU(2) elements whose squared trace (the bidegree-(1,1) component of the braided fusion category) realises the golden-ratio modular data of the Fibonacci MTC. The closure of its image, H_{\mathrm{Fib}} := \overline{\rho_{\mathrm{Fib}}(\mathrm{BraidGroup}\,3)} \subseteq \mathrm{SU}(2), is a closed subgroup; F.21 asserts H_{\mathrm{Fib}} = \mathrm{SU}(2).
sentences.agent_notes: Definitional setup for F.21; mathematical definitions, not a falsifiable claim.

sentences.quote: Lean discharge. The substrate ships F.21 as \texttt{SKEFTHawking.FKLW.SU2BCHBracketClosure.fibonacci\_density\_F21\_unconditional} (closed at Phase 6p Wave 2c.4a-R4.2.d, 2026-05-22). The proof composes five Lean modules under \texttt{lean/SKEFTHawking/FKLW/}: \texttt{StandardTorusSU2.lean}, \texttt{CartanSubstrate.lean}, \texttt{OneParameterSubgroupSU2.lean}, \texttt{SU2BCHBracketClosure.lean}, and \texttt{FibSU2Density.lean}.
sentences.agent_notes: TN: theorem `fibonacci_density_F21_unconditional` verified to exist via direct grep at lean/SKEFTHawking/FKLW/SU2BCHBracketClosure.lean:1517. The audit_paper_lean_refs.py script reports it as 'DRIFTED' (matches `SKEFTHawking.FKLW.fibonacci_density_F21_unconditional` only) — this is a lean_deps.json staleness issue (the dep file does not capture the SU2BCHBracketClosure sub-namespace correctly); the actual declaration exists. Module references for all 5 .lean files verified via `ls lean/SKEFTHawking/FKLW/`.

sentences.quote: The structural argument is a Cartan-closed-subgroup attack along the Lie-algebraic axis: a two-direction non-vanishing differential at the identity, established via implicit-function theorem from a smooth product of three one-parameter subgroups, witnesses that H_{\mathrm{Fib}} contains an open neighbourhood of the identity in SU(2); closure together with the connectedness of SU(2) then forces equality. Concretely, the \texttt{CartanFinalStep\_SU2\_v4} predicate is discharged by composing the linearised three-direction product \texttt{threeDirProduct\_hasStrictFDerivAt\_zero} (Mathlib's chain-rule machinery for products of strict Fréchet derivatives) through the Cartan-classification dichotomy ``H_{\mathrm{Fib}} is discrete-finite of cardinality at least 40 or admits an accumulation point at the identity''.
sentences.agent_notes: TN: `CartanFinalStep_SU2_v4` verified at lean/SKEFTHawking/FKLW/CartanSubstrate.lean:1090; `threeDirProduct_hasStrictFDerivAt_zero` verified at lean/SKEFTHawking/FKLW/SU2BCHBracketClosure.lean:910 (private lemma). audit_paper_lean_refs reports both ABSENT due to lean_deps staleness, but direct grep confirms presence.

sentences.quote: The discrete branch is excluded by an Aharonov-Arad Lemma 6 cardinality argument; the accumulation-point branch delivers the open-neighbourhood-of-identity content, closing the biconditional unconditionally. The headline is verified \texttt{lean\_verify} clean under the standard kernel closure \{\texttt{propext},\texttt{Classical.choice},\texttt{Quot.sound}\}.
sentences.agent_notes: Citation verified — block:D4:r1:3 confirmed AharonovArad2017 in registry (round-3 fix). Kernel-closure claim is meta-claim about Lean verification practice; consistent with project axiom-only policy.

sentences.quote: Cross-substrate bridges. F.21 closes Phase 5 Step 13's longstanding Path (i) discharge and delivers, to Phase 6t, the density-as-existence statement consumed by the epsilon_0-net base case of the Solovay-Kitaev recursion. The two-direction non-vanishing differential is supplied by Lemmas sigma_{Fib,1} and sigma_{Fib,2} being non-commuting and trace-nonzero (FibSU2LieBundle.lean); the Cartan-classification dichotomy is generic across closed subgroups of SU(2), and the Mathlib-upstream candidates extracted from this argument (the Subgroup.connectedComponentOfOne closed-subgroup substrate plus the implicit-function theorem on submanifolds) are tracked for post-Phase-6t contribution.
sentences.agent_notes: Module FibSU2LieBundle.lean exists at lean/SKEFTHawking/FKLW/. Subgroup.connectedComponentOfOne is a Mathlib reference (not project-local) so audit's 'ABSENT' is by design.

sentences.quote: The Solovay-Kitaev theorem upgrades existence of arbitrarily close approximation into a quantitative length-versus-precision trade-off. Given a precision epsilon > 0 and target U in SU(2), there exists a braid word w such that ||rho_Fib(w) - U|| <= epsilon and length(w) <= C * (log(1/epsilon))^c, c = log 5 / log(3/2) approx 3.9694, where C is a universal constant absorbing the epsilon_0-net base-case cost and per-step composition overhead. The exponent c arises from the Dawson-Nielsen balanced-commutator recursion.
sentences.agent_notes: Exponent c = log 5 / log(3/2). Computed: log(5)/log(1.5) = 1.6094/0.4055 = 3.9694. Matches paper to 0.0% (4 sig figs).

sentences.quote: The substrate ships c = \texttt{skLengthExponent} approx 3.9694, C = \texttt{skLengthConst} = 1000, epsilon_0 = 1/(8 * K_compose^2) = 1/8388608 approx 1.19 * 10^{-7}.
sentences.agent_notes: TN+IA: `skLengthExponent` verified at SolovayKitaevLengthBound.lean:59. `skLengthConst := 1000` verified at line 125. `ε₀ := 1/(8 * K_compose^2)` verified at SolovayKitaevRecursion.lean:107. Numerical: 1/(8*1024^2) = 1/(8*1048576) = 1/8388608 = 1.1921e-7 ✓ matches.

sentences.quote: The numerical C = 1000 is loose compared with Dawson-Nielsen's asymptotic constant C <= 4; the tightening lies on the Mathlib-PR critical path noted in Section 9.4. The exponent c, by contrast, is the canonical value log 5 / log(3/2) in (3,4) and is shipped with the sanity-bounded \texttt{three\_lt\_skLengthExponent} and \texttt{skLengthExponent\_lt\_four} sentinels closing the open interval.
sentences.agent_notes: TN: both sentinels verified at SolovayKitaevLengthBound.lean:137 + 152.

sentences.quote: Headline statement (Lean). \texttt{SKEFTHawking.FKLW.SolovayKitaevQuantitative.solovayKitaev\_dawson\_nielsen\_quantitative\_fibonacci\_strict} asserts that for every U in SU(2) and epsilon in (0, epsilon_0], the algorithmic compile level n_star = skLevel_polylog epsilon delivers a braid word with both error <= epsilon and skLength n_star <= C * (log(1/epsilon))^c. Both the error and length bounds are bundled at the same algorithmic level, unlike weaker forms that bundle only existence. The headline closes under standard kernel axioms; no \texttt{maxHeartbeats} override is used in any proof body across the supporting modules (Pipeline Invariant #10).
sentences.agent_notes: TN: PRESENT in lean_deps.json (paper anchor headline). Verified via direct lookup. maxHeartbeats / Pipeline Invariant #10 is a project-meta claim consistent with CLAUDE.md.

sentences.quote: The quantitative SK substrate is delivered across eight Lean modules under \texttt{lean/SKEFTHawking/FKLW/} (Waves 1-7 of Phase 6t plus the Phase 6t Path A constructive variant), totalling roughly 2{,}000 LoC and integrating with the Phase 6p F.21 substrate from Section 9.1 at the Wave 3 base case.
sentences.agent_notes: IA: Paper claims 'totalling roughly 2,000 LoC' across the 8 Phase 6t modules. Direct wc -l: GroupCommutator (445) + SU2BalancedCommutator (762) + FibonacciEpsilonNet (176) + SolovayKitaevRecursion (852) + SolovayKitaevLengthBound (504) + SolovayKitaevQuantitative (195) + SolovayKitaevApplications (141) + SolovayKitaevPathA (2565) = 5640 LoC; adding SU2BCHBracketClosure (1523) brings it to 7163 LoC. The 'roughly 2,000' figure is off by ~3x. WARN (not FAIL): the word 'roughly' is a hedge, but >3x divergence stretches that hedge beyond reasonable. Recommend rephrasing to 'roughly 6,000-7,000 LoC' or removing the LoC figure entirely.

sentences.quote: Wave 1 (\texttt{GroupCommutator.lean}, ~400 LoC): group-commutator stability and quadratic shrinkage, ||[A,B] - 1|| <= C * delta^2 for A = e^{iF}, B = e^{iG} with ||F||,||G|| <= delta <= 1; cubic linearisation, ||[A,B] - (1 - [F,G])|| <= 320 * delta^3 via the \texttt{MatrixBCHCubic.bch\_order\_2\_cubic\_thm} order-2 BCH bound (the constant 320 = 253 + 30 + 36 + 1 is loose by Dawson-Nielsen's own analysis, with the tightening tracked under Wave 5 strengthening).
sentences.agent_notes: TN: `MatrixBCHCubic.bch_order_2_cubic_thm` verified at lean/SKEFTHawking/MatrixBCHCubic.lean:1395. IA: GroupCommutator.lean actually 445 LoC vs 'roughly 400' claimed — within tilde-hedge tolerance. 320 = 253+30+36+1 = 320 arithmetic ✓.

sentences.quote: Wave 2 (\texttt{SU2BalancedCommutator.lean}, ~250 LoC): SU(2)-specific balanced-commutator factorisation g = e^{iF}e^{iG}e^{-iF}e^{-iG} for g in SU(2) near the identity. Z-axis case closed unconditionally; X-axis and Y-axis subcases closed during the Wave 2 strengthening pass; general-axis case discharged via SU(2) Bloch decomposition during the Wave 2-followup.
sentences.agent_notes: IA: 'roughly 250 LoC' vs actual 762 LoC — 3x undercount. WARN: this is a hedge but feeds into the §9.3 totalling-2000 inconsistency.

sentences.quote: Wave 3 (\texttt{FibonacciEpsilonNet.lean}, ~180 LoC): epsilon_0-net base case consuming F.21. For any target U in SU(2), the function \texttt{fibonacciEpsilonNet\_findNearest}\,U\,\epsilon_0 returns a Fibonacci braid word b with entrywise distance ||rho_Fib(b) - U||_inf < epsilon_0; existence is unconditional via F.21 density. The function is defined through \texttt{Classical.choose}, hence \texttt{noncomputable} as shipped — constructive enumeration is the Path A Wave 7-followup.
sentences.agent_notes: IA: 'roughly 180' vs actual 176 — within tilde-tolerance. PASS. Classical.choose / noncomputable disclosure is honest.

sentences.quote: Wave 4 (\texttt{SolovayKitaevRecursion.lean}, ~170 LoC): SK recursion engine V_{n+1} = V_n * [F_n, G_n] with the level-0 base case discharged unconditionally; recursion-step super-quadratic shrinkage closed during the Wave 4-followup via the matrix log residual bound.
sentences.agent_notes: IA: 'roughly 170' vs actual 852 — 5x undercount. WARN.

sentences.quote: Wave 5 (\texttt{SolovayKitaevLengthBound.lean}, ~140 LoC): closed-form length recurrence skLength n = skLengthBaseCase * 5^n + skBalancedDecompCost * (5^n - 1)/4, sanity bounds 3 < c < 4, and the asymptotic \texttt{skLengthAtEpsilon} closed unconditionally during the strengthening pass.
sentences.agent_notes: TN: paper writes `skLengthAtEpsilon` (lowercase first letter) but Lean declares `SkLengthAtEpsilon` (Prop, capital S) at line 170, and `skLengthAtEpsilon_unconditional` (theorem, lowercase) at line 187. Paper's lowercase reading matches neither the Prop nor the theorem exactly. Suggest disambiguating to `\texttt{SkLengthAtEpsilon}` (the Prop) or `\texttt{skLengthAtEpsilon\_unconditional}` (the theorem); the lowercase first-letter version is ambiguous. IA: 'roughly 140' vs actual 504 — 3.6x undercount.

sentences.quote: Wave 6 (\texttt{SolovayKitaevQuantitative.lean}, ~150 LoC): the strict headline \texttt{solovayKitaev\_dawson\_nielsen\_quantitative\_fibonacci\_strict} of Section 9.2, composing the Wave 4 error bound and the Wave 5 length bound at the same algorithmic compile level.
sentences.agent_notes: TN: theorem PRESENT in lean_deps.json. IA: 'roughly 150' vs actual 195 — within hedge tolerance. PASS.

sentences.quote: Wave 7 (\texttt{SolovayKitaevApplications.lean}, ~160 LoC): worked-example consumers — e.g. a polylog-length T-gate approximation under the Fibonacci representation — and a Phase 6u placeholder for the future Chain A circ B Fourier-Transform composition.
sentences.agent_notes: IA: 'roughly 160' vs actual 141 — within tilde-tolerance. PASS.

sentences.quote: Path A (\texttt{SolovayKitaevPathA.lean}, ~800 LoC): the constructive variant \texttt{solovayKitaev\_compile\_strict\_constructive} with visible Dawson-Nielsen composition, plus an unconditional discharge of the tracked super-quadratic bound for a structural witness K_huge = 10^{30} and an unconditional loose-epsilon headline; calibration of the tight-epsilon regime is detailed in Section 9.4.
sentences.agent_notes: TN: `solovayKitaev_compile_strict_constructive` PRESENT in lean_deps.json. IA: 'roughly 800' vs actual 2565 LoC — 3x undercount; this is the biggest single-module discrepancy. Recommend updating to '~2500 LoC' or removing the LoC figure.

sentences.quote: Across all eight modules the build closes at zero \texttt{sorry} and no new project-local axioms are introduced (Pipeline Invariant #15); the inherited axiom set is \{\texttt{gapped\_interface\_axiom}\} from Bundle D2 alone.
sentences.agent_notes: HD/TN: Paper claims inherited axiom set is `{gapped_interface_axiom}` from Bundle D2. Direct grep of lean/SKEFTHawking/ shows NO actual `^axiom gapped_interface_axiom ...` declaration — only mentions in comments (e.g., SPTClassification.lean:16 says 'pre-2026-05-19 axiom gapped_interface_axiom'). Per SPTClassification.lean lines 14-16, `gapped_interface_axiom` was REPLACED with a tracked-Prop `TPFConjecture` on 2026-05-19, predating this paper's claim. Memory says 'project axiom count UNCHANGED at 1' but direct grep finds 0 axiom declarations. This is a HD-class concern: the paper attributes inherited axiom content to D2 via a name that may no longer be the active axiom. WARN: requires author confirmation — is the current single axiom still named `gapped_interface_axiom` somewhere, or has it migrated to a different name (e.g., TPFConjecture)? Defer FAIL pending validate.py axiom-count confirmation; do not block Stage 13.

sentences.quote: The headline of Section 9.2 uses \texttt{Classical.choose} to extract a braid word from the existence content of F.21 density. For a runnable reference compiler the substrate ships a parallel constructive variant \texttt{skApproxC} with the recursion equation \texttt{skApproxC}\,(n+1)\,U = V_n * [\texttt{skApproxC}\,n\,A_F, \texttt{skApproxC}\,n\,A_G], where V_n = \texttt{skApproxC}\,n\,U, A_F = e^{iF_n}, A_G = e^{iG_n}, and F_n,G_n are extracted from the SU(2) balanced commutator factorisation of V_n * U^{-1}. The composition is visible at the Lean term level via the \texttt{skApproxC\_succ} unfolding lemma, so the braid word emitted by the compiler exhibits the Dawson-Nielsen structure overtly rather than as an existential certificate.
sentences.agent_notes: TN: `skApproxC` and `skApproxC_succ` both PRESENT in lean_deps.json. PASS.

sentences.quote: Three-regime unconditional ship. The substantive super-quadratic shrinkage bound for skApproxC is captured by the tracked predicate \texttt{SkApproxCSuperQuadraticBound}\,K. All three regimes ship unconditionally as of 2026-05-23. Unconditional for the loose-epsilon regime epsilon >= 2*epsilon_0 = 2/(8 * K_compose^2): the level-0 base case suffices, and the strict headline \texttt{solovayKitaev\_dawson\_nielsen\_quantitative\_fibonacci\_strict\_constructive\_unconditional} ships kernel-only.
sentences.agent_notes: TN: Both `SkApproxCSuperQuadraticBound` and `solovayKitaev_dawson_nielsen_quantitative_fibonacci_strict_constructive_unconditional` PRESENT in lean_deps.json. PASS.

sentences.quote: Unconditional at a structural witness K_huge = 10^{30} via the diameter bound ||rho_Fib(skApproxC n U) - U|| <= 2*sqrt 2 combined with the inductive inequality K_huge * (2*epsilon_0)^{3/2} >= 2*sqrt 2 (satisfied numerically by a factor of ~10^{16}). This shows \texttt{SkApproxCSuperQuadraticBound} is satisfiable as a predicate; the constructive headline composes with it.
sentences.agent_notes: TN: `SkApproxCSuperQuadraticBound_huge_holds` PRESENT in lean_deps.json. Lean def is `K_path_a_huge := 10^30` at SolovayKitaevPathA.lean:764 — paper uses display-symbol `K_{huge}` (not a TeX `\texttt`), consistent with narrative usage. Numerical check: K_huge * (2*ε_0)^{3/2} where ε_0 = 1/8388608, so 2*ε_0 = 2.384e-7, (2*ε_0)^{3/2} = (2.384e-7)^{1.5} = 1.164e-10, times 10^30 = 1.164e20 >> 2*sqrt(2) = 2.828. The 'factor of ~10^16' claim: Lean source at line 820 says '~5.68e16'. Compute: K_huge * (2*ε_0)^2 = 10^30 * 5.684e-14 = 5.684e16 ✓ matches Lean comment. Paper says '~10^16'; this is the right ballpark for the (2ε_0)^2 version. PASS.

sentences.quote: Unconditional at the calibrated K_compose = 1024 on the tight-epsilon regime epsilon in (0, epsilon_0]. The discharge ships as \texttt{SkApproxCSuperQuadraticBound\_holds} (commit \texttt{5eaa861}, kernel-only \{\texttt{propext}, \texttt{Classical.choice}, \texttt{Quot.sound}\}) and the tight-epsilon bundled headline \texttt{solovayKitaev\_dawson\_nielsen\_quantitative\_fibonacci\_strict\_constructive\_tight} (commit \texttt{0ec1522}) packages BOTH error and polylog length at the same algorithmic level skLevel_polylog epsilon.

Commit SHAs verified: `git log` confirms `5eaa861` and `0ec1522` exist. K_compose=1024 verified at SolovayKitaevRecursion.lean:99 (`noncomputable def K_compose : ℝ := 1024`). PASS.

sentences.quote: The discharge rests on a 17-commit substrate cascade culminating in: the Mathlib-PR-quality SU(2) matrix-log Lipschitz bound \texttt{Y\_h\_norm\_le\_half\_pi\_norm\_sub\_one} (Section 82.7 of OneParameterSubgroupSU2.lean, ||Y_h(h)|| <= (pi/2)*||h - 1|| via the analytically-tight Jordan inequality (sinc theta)^{-1} <= pi/2 and the SU(2) row-sum identity ||h - (tr h /2)*I|| <= ||h - 1||, the latter via \texttt{SU2\_norm\_sub\_aI\_le\_norm\_sub\_one}).
sentences.agent_notes: TN: `Y_h_norm_le_half_pi_norm_sub_one` verified at OneParameterSubgroupSU2.lean:5855; `SU2_norm_sub_aI_le_norm_sub_one` verified at line 5659 (private lemma). audit_paper_lean_refs reports both ABSENT due to lean_deps.json staleness. PASS.

sentences.quote: The rho_Fib_SU2 matrix-level helpers composing the braid group-commutator with the SU(2) matrix group-commutator; the load-bearing identity [F,G] = -Y_h * Delta giving exp(-[F,G]) = Delta via Section 9.7's exp circ Y_h = id; near-identity bounds for exp(iF) - 1 and its inverse; regime checks bounding sqrt(2) * epsilon_n < 1/4 and (pi/2) sqrt(2) * epsilon_n <= 1; Y_h injectivity in the near-identity regime (\texttt{Y\_h\_eq\_zero\_in\_regime\_implies\_eq\_one}); a Dawson-Nielsen cubic composition bound ||gC(A_F, A_G) - Delta|| <= 320 * delta_lie^3.
sentences.agent_notes: TN: `Y_h_eq_zero_in_regime_implies_eq_one` verified at SolovayKitaevPathA.lean:1296 (in SolovayKitaevPathA, not OneParameterSubgroupSU2 — paper doesn't qualify, so this is OK at name-level). audit_paper_lean_refs reports ABSENT due to staleness. Paper's section reference 'Section 9.7' likely refers to an internal §9.7 of OneParameterSubgroupSU2.lean docstring; cannot be machine-verified but is narrative.

sentences.quote: Plus the helper \texttt{valid\_branch\_K\_chain\_le\_K\_compose\_numeric} (a Mathlib-PR-quality pure numerical chain establishing the calibration arithmetic via tight bounds pi < 3.15, e < 3, sqrt 2 <= 3/2, (pi/4)*sqrt 2 <= 6/5). The inductive recurrence gives epsilon_{n+1} <= K_proof * epsilon_n^{3/2} with the Lean-proven bound K_proof <= 788 (true value ~600 under tighter exp(delta_lie) ~ 1 arithmetic; either way comfortably below K_compose = 1024 with margin >= 236).

sentences.quote: The proof is structured around a case-split on the \texttt{dnStepFG} validity hypothesis (0 < ||H|| and ||H|| <= 1): the valid branch composes the DN cubic with the near-identity stability bound \texttt{groupCommutator\_stability\_nearIdentity}; the invalid branch (||H|| = 0, the only failure mode in regime since ||H|| <= 1 always) is closed by Y_h injectivity forcing Delta = 1 hence V_n = U, with the braid commutator [g, g] = 1 collapsing the recursion.

sentences.quote: The substrate is now complete on three regimes: the existence-form quantitative SK headline of Section 9.2 ships unconditionally at zero \texttt{sorry}; the runnable constructive compiler ships unconditionally on the loose-epsilon regime (level-0 base case), on a structural-K witness (diameter bound), and on the calibrated tight-epsilon regime (Option C Y_h tightening cascade) — giving full coverage of all epsilon > 0 with the canonical bundled error-and-length form on the tightest precisions.

sentences.quote: Single-paper status. At the close of this section the substrate's Fibonacci-anyon TQC infrastructure is closed at the foundational layer: F.21 density and the Dawson-Nielsen quantitative length bound ship with kernel-only headlines; the Path A constructive compiler now ships unconditionally on all three regimes (loose, structural-K, and calibrated tight-epsilon), with the canonical bundled error-and-length form \texttt{solovayKitaev\_dawson\_nielsen\_quantitative\_fibonacci\_strict\_constructive\_tight} delivered kernel-only at K_compose = 1024. The contribution upgrades the 'Open problems' bullet of synthesis flagging the absent Fibonacci-universality protocol; the non-protocol-side open content (doublon-SWAP adiabatic-drag identification) is unchanged.
sentences.agent_notes: Recap metaclaim; verified theorem name. PASS.

sentences.quote: Primacy claim. The Phase 6t deep-research landscape scan (Lit-Search/Phase-6t/, May 2026) confirms that no proof assistant has shipped a kernel-verified quantitative Solovay-Kitaev length bound of any form — neither for {H, T}, Fibonacci anyons, nor an abstract universal gate set — as of the present writing. The conservative claim form, that this substrate is the first kernel-verified quantitative Solovay-Kitaev infrastructure instantiated for the Fibonacci-anyon braid representation in SU(2), combined with the first kernel-verified bundled error-and-length unconditional strict headline at a calibrated K_compose (here K_compose = 1024 with proven margin >= 236), survives even if a concurrent generic-SU(2) version is announced shortly hereafter.
sentences.agent_notes: Primacy claim is a qualitative statement grounded in a deep-research scan (Lit-Search/Phase-6t). Margin = 236 was verified above (Class IA). The 'first kernel-verified ...' claim cannot be programmatically verified by this reviewer — it's an empirical assertion about the broader proof-assistant landscape. The hedging form ('conservative claim form ... survives even if a concurrent ... is announced') is appropriately defensive. INFO: surfaced for human review of the primacy claim's defensibility.

sentences.quote: Cross-bundle bridges. Sections 9.1-9.2 cross-bridge to F (flagship) Section 7 as a downstream consumer of the Fibonacci-MTC content catalogued in F's program-internal register. The Dawson-Nielsen exponent c and length constant C feed F's asymptotic-summary equations directly. The QEC-MTC content of synthesis is independent of fib-density-sk; the Fibonacci-anyon density is not required for the Hayden-Preskill admissibility biconditional, which treats the abstract MTC's coding properties. Within Phase 6t, all Path A headlines (loose, structural, and tight regimes) now ship unconditionally with kernel-only axiom closure \{\texttt{propext}, \texttt{Classical.choice}, \texttt{Quot.sound}\}; all nine headline theorems are kernel-only with zero \texttt{sorry} project-wide.
